cases.title: Turn a math video into a study plan
cases.scenario: A learner uploads a short mock screenshot from a math lesson and asks what to do next.

input.userMessage: I watched this lesson but still do not know how to practice. Break it into steps.
constraints: Start with the most likely missing concept.
Do not assume access to the original video.

orchestration.summary: Identify the concept gap, create a short review pass, then generate practice and self-check steps.

tasks.title: Identify the target concept
tasks.description: Extract the lesson topic and likely prerequisite from the screenshot context.

tasks.title: Create a focused review
tasks.description: Explain the concept using one compact worked example before assigning practice.

tasks.title: Generate practice questions
tasks.description: Create three progressively harder practice items and one self-check answer key.

tasks.title: Ask learner to self-check
tasks.description: Pause for the learner to solve the items and report which step failed.

cases.responsePreview: I will turn the screenshot into a 30-minute study path: identify the concept, review one example, practice three items, then self-check.

cases.title: Clarify an ambiguous homework request
cases.scenario: A user sends an unclear math prompt and expects the system to avoid guessing.

input.userMessage: Can you solve this and explain it? The question says simplify the expression, but I am not sure what expression.
constraints: Ask for missing information before solving.
Offer examples of what the user can send next.

orchestration.summary: Detect missing input, ask a targeted clarification question, and prepare the solver workflow after the user responds.

tasks.title: Detect missing expression
tasks.description: Confirm that the core math expression is absent from the request.

tasks.title: Ask for the expression
tasks.description: Request the exact expression or a screenshot before attempting a solution.

tasks.title: Prepare solver path
tasks.description: Queue the solve-and-explain workflow for when the missing input arrives.

cases.responsePreview: I need the expression first. Send the exact text or a clear screenshot, then I can simplify it step by step.

cases.title: Plan a research brief from a broad request
cases.scenario: A founder asks for a short competitive research brief with clear execution steps.

input.userMessage: Make a quick brief about AI scheduling assistants for small teams. I need the key angles, not a long report.
constraints: Use source notes separately from the final brief.

orchestration.summary: Scope the market question, collect source notes, synthesize product angles, and draft a concise brief.

tasks.title: Scope the research question
tasks.description: Define target buyer, comparison set, and output constraints.

tasks.title: Collect source notes
tasks.description: Gather current notes from official pages and credible product materials.

tasks.title: Synthesize key angles
tasks.description: Group findings into buyer pain, product promise, workflow fit, and adoption risk.

tasks.title: Draft the brief
tasks.description: Write an under-800-word brief with assumptions and source-note references.

cases.responsePreview: I will first narrow the buyer and comparison set, then gather source notes before drafting the short brief.

cases.title: Review an orchestration board
cases.scenario: A product reviewer inspects a mock task board and asks what is blocked.

input.userMessage: Look at this board and tell me which tasks need attention first.
constraints: Do not invent owners beyond the visible labels.

orchestration.summary: Read visible board state, rank blocked items, then produce the next action list.

tasks.title: Read board state
tasks.description: Extract visible columns, owners, and blocked markers from the screenshot.

tasks.title: Rank attention items
tasks.description: Prioritize blocked work before ready or in-progress tasks.

tasks.title: Write action list
tasks.description: Return a concise list of owner-specific next actions.

cases.responsePreview: The blocked items should be handled first, followed by waiting user decisions and then normal ready tasks.
